blurb:
This place absolutely lives up to the hype and then some. The vibe is vibrant and welcoming, and the service is on point. Perfect for girls' night out or when you want Mexican food that's actually exciting.

Start with the Esquite because it's packed with flavor and sets the tone perfectly. The Pineapple Guava pitcher and Passion Fruit pitcher are both refreshing and perfectly balanced between sweet and boozy. They're great for sharing at the table.

Open until midnight most nights which is amazing when you need good food late. It gets busy but the energy is part of the fun.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
I'm not usually a thin-crust person, but this place converted me. It's authentic Italian style with ingredients that actually taste like something. Great for date night or when you want pizza that feels a little more grown-up than the usual spots.

Their margherita is perfect if you want to taste what real pizza should be like. The crust gets those perfect leopard spots and doesn't flop over when you pick it up. They also do really good salads and antipasti if you want to make it a full meal.

It gets busy on weekends so I'd call ahead. The Blue Stone Road location is easy to get to and has decent parking.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This is officially in my top 5 dinner places in Atlanta, and I'm not being dramatic. One of my girlfriends dragged me here for girls' night out and I literally cannot wait to go back. The whole experience is just perfect from start to finish.

Get the hummus and wings to start, then go for the chicken shish kabobs with the rice that has lentils and raisins. The cocktails are all good so just pick whatever sounds appealing. Save room for the tiramisu because it's worth every calorie.

They break between lunch and dinner every day, so don't show up at 3pm expecting to eat. But honestly, this is special occasion dining that's worth planning around.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This food hall is perfect when your family can't agree on what to eat. It's right near the square, so you can grab food and then walk around downtown Marietta afterward. Twenty different vendors means everyone can get exactly what they want and still sit together.

The variety is impressive. You've got everything from tacos to pizza to Asian food all under one roof. It's especially great for lunch meetings or when you have out-of-town visitors who want to try a little bit of everything. The setup makes it easy to order from different places and meet back at a table.

Parking can be tight on weekends when the square is busy, but it's worth it. The hours are decent too, staying open until 9pm most nights and 10pm on weekends. Great for a casual dinner when you don't want to commit to one cuisine.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Sun-Thu 11:00 AM â 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM â 10:00 PM

blurb:
This is my go-to when I'm craving really good biryani without the fuss. It's in Perimeter Center Place and feels like eating at a friend's house, just with way better cooking. Perfect for families or when you want to introduce someone to Indian food who might be intimidated by spicier places.

The biryani is obviously what you come for, and it's legit. They serve everything family-style, which is great when you're with a group and want to try a bunch of different things. The colorful decor is cheerful without being over the top.

Open late on weekends which is clutch when you need good food after 9pm. Just know they break between lunch and dinner some days, so don't count on a random 3pm meal.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA

blurb:
This little spot in Franklin Gateway has become our go-to when we want fresh, flavorful Middle Eastern food without the fuss. It's casual and family-friendly, and the prices are really reasonable. Great for a quick lunch or picking up dinner on the way home from work.

The shawarma and falafel are what most people come for, and both are really well done. They also have good vegetarian options, which is nice when you're trying to please everyone in the family. The portions are generous and everything tastes fresh.

They're open seven days a week until 9pm, which makes it easy to fit into your schedule. The staff is friendly and patient if you're new to Middle Eastern food and need recommendations.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
This is where we go for anniversaries or when my parents are in town and want somewhere special. The tables right on the Chattahoochee River are gorgeous, especially at sunset. It's pricey but worth it for the kind of occasion where you want to feel fancy.

The Southern seafood is what they do best. I always get something with their Georgia trout or shrimp and grits when it's on the menu. The atmosphere is intimate without being stuffy, and the service makes you feel like you're the only table there.

Make a reservation and ask for a river table if the weather's nice. Sunday brunch is popular but they close between 4 and 5pm, so plan accordingly.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
This is the diner we all wish we had in our neighborhood. It's been feeding Marietta families 24/7 for decades, and the huge neon sign makes it impossible to miss on Cobb Parkway. Perfect for those late-night cravings, early morning meetings, or when you just need comfort food that hits the spot.

The menu is absolutely massive, but don't let that intimidate you. The breakfast is what they're really known for, and it's available all day. Their Greek specialties are solid too, which makes sense since it's been Greek-owned forever. The portions are generous and the prices won't break the bank.

Fair warning: parking can be tricky during peak hours, and you might wait a bit on weekends. But that's kind of part of the charm of a place that's been this popular for this long.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
This is hands down the best French bakery in the area. The owner is actually French, and you can tell the difference in everything they make. It's bright and welcoming, perfect for a weekend brunch or picking up pastries for a special occasion. The kind of place that makes you feel a little more sophisticated just walking in.

The baguettes are as good as you'll find outside of Paris, and the quiches are perfect for a light lunch. Their petits fours are gorgeous and actually taste as good as they look, which isn't always a given with fancy desserts. The croissants are flaky and buttery, exactly like they should be.

They close early on Sundays and don't stay open super late during the week, so plan accordingly. But honestly, this is morning and afternoon food anyway. Great for impressing houseguests or treating yourself to something special.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM â 6:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM â 3:30 PM

blurb:
If you've never tried Brazilian food, this is a great place to start. It's all-you-can-eat, which sounds scary but it's actually perfect for trying different things without committing to a huge plate of something you might not like. The atmosphere is relaxed and family-friendly.

The barbecue meats are the main event, and they keep bringing different cuts to your table until you say stop. But honestly, the cheese bread alone is worth the trip. They also serve feijoada stew, which is like the ultimate comfort food if you want something hearty and warming.

They're open for both lunch and dinner, and they even do breakfast. It's in the Powers Ferry shopping area, so parking is easy. Just come hungry because you're definitely going to eat more than you planned.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Mon-Thu, Sun 10:00 AM â 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM â 11:00 PM
